response:
SECTION 1  
the two tree hollows in a cedar  
a squirrel sitting in front of the second hollow  
said:  
1) there are no nuts in the other hollow  
2) there is at least one nut in one of the hollows  

SECTION 2  
red squirrels always tell the truth  
gray squirrels always lie  
what color is this squirrel  

SECTION 3  
analyze the statements  
statement 1: no nuts in the other hollow  
statement 2: at least one nut in one of the hollows  

SECTION 4  
determine the logical consistency  
if statement 1 is true, then statement 2 must also be true  
if statement 1 is false, then statement 2 must also be false  

SECTION 5  
evaluate the implications of the statements  
if the squirrel is red, both statements must be true  
if the squirrel is gray, both statements must be false  

SECTION 6  
conclude the color of the squirrel  
the squirrel is gray  
the squirrel is gray
